hypothesis: A verified S&P 500 addition announcement predicts a positive market-hedged residual return from the next executable open through the effective date -- the announcement->effective continuation ('index effect'). Independent of the reversal test (rejected) and the effective-day event study (null). Whether it survives recently, after costs, is unknown and is what this test answers.

strategy.signal: Verified S&P 500 addition announcement (Tier 1/2/3, exact time).
strategy.entry: Next regular-session OPEN after announcement_public_time -- even for intraday announcements. Conservative and executable with daily bars.
strategy.hedge: Stock leg market-hedged with the frozen pre-event beta vs SPY (same 60d pre-event daily-return beta, clipped [0,3], as Track A).
strategy.costs: Paid on BOTH stock and hedge legs, at entry AND exit (15 bps/side baseline).
strategy.position: Long the added name, short beta*SPY. Equal-weight within an announcement batch.

inference.controls: Matched non-added controls (momentum + dollar-ADV, uncontaminated by nearby index changes), and placebo announcements (shifted announcement dates).
inference.separation: Raw event-study residuals reported separately from after-cost portfolio returns.

selection_bias_guard: Compare RESOLVED vs UNRESOLVED batches by year, event type (quarterly/ad-hoc) and number of additions, so source availability does not silently create a selected sample. Report this before any outcome.
kill_gates: The scoreboard kill gates apply (>=100 indep events after cost, Sharpe>1, delayed+ and doubled-cost+, bootstrap/HAC LB>0, no dominant ticker/batch, paper-forward+). Falling short of the sample thresholds downgrades the CLAIM (full/underpowered/pilot), never relaxes the gates.
blindness: No returns, price charts, or rule changes based on coverage until the fixed denominator (all 113 batches) is resolved-or-unresolved and the announcement manifest is frozen.
